info.title: Norien Registry API
info.description: Agent registry and tool marketplace. Publish agents and reusable tools, discover them through ranked search, and install them by slug and version.

apiKey.name: authorization
apiKey.description: Reserved for a later phase. Not enforced yet.

runtime.description: Execution runtime. Inferred from the entrypoint extension when omitted.

entrypoint.description: Module, script, or binary that starts the agent.

tools.description: Slugs of tools this agent requires.

start.description: Command that starts the agent. Derived from the runtime when omitted.

health.description: Command or HTTP path that reports agent health.

commands.description: Lifecycle commands. Recorded now; executed in a later phase.

examples.name: Research Agent
examples.description: Searches the web and summarises findings with citations.

missing.description: Declared tools with no published implementation.
Required variables the caller has not supplied.

ready.description: True when dependencies and environment are both satisfied.

NormalizedAgentInput.description: The platform's understanding of an agent: detected runtime, resolved tools, environment readiness, and version status. Nothing is executed.

agent.description: Slug of the agent to install.

version.description: Exact version or semver range. Defaults to latest.

environment.description: Environment variable names (or a name/value map) available to the agent.

examples.description: Searches the web and summarises findings with citations.

missing.description: Declared tools with no published implementation.
Required variables the caller has not supplied.

ready.description: True when dependencies and environment are both satisfied.

NormalizedAgent.description: The platform's understanding of an agent: detected runtime, resolved tools, environment readiness, and version status. Nothing is executed.

agent.description: Slug of the agent to install.

version.description: Exact version or semver range. Defaults to latest.

environment.description: Environment variable names (or a name/value map) available to the agent.

get.description: Liveness and dependency check. Returns 503 with `status: degraded` when the database is unreachable, so a load balancer can act on it.
Paginated catalogue of agents. Supports full-text search, tag and author filters, and filtering by required tool.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Repeat or comma-separate to filter by several tags.

parameters.name: tool
parameters.description: Only agents that require this tool.

post.description: Creates an agent and its first version. Accepts either a manifest or explicit fields; explicit fields take precedence. Fails with 409 if the slug is taken -- use POST /publish to add a version.

runtime.description: Execution runtime. Inferred from the entrypoint extension when omitted.

start.description: Command that starts the agent. Derived from the runtime when omitted.

health.description: Command or HTTP path that reports agent health.

commands.description: Lifecycle commands. Recorded now; executed in a later phase.

get.description: Returns the agent at its latest version, or at a specific version/range via `?version=`.

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Exact version or range. Defaults to the latest published version.

patch.description: Updates presentation and distribution metadata. Published version payloads are immutable -- publish a new version to change them.

delete.description: Soft-deletes the agent. The slug stays reserved so previously published references can never be repointed.

get.summary: List published versions of an agent

get.description: Paginated tool catalogue with category, runtime, tag, and author filters.

parameters.name: runtime
parameters.description: How the tool executes: node, python, or http.

post.description: Creates a tool and its first version from a tool.json manifest. Fails with 409 if the slug is taken -- use POST /tools/publish to add a version.

runtime.description: How the tool executes: node, python, or http.

input_schema.description: A JSON Schema document describing the payload shape.

output_schema.description: A JSON Schema document describing the payload shape.

get.description: Ranked full-text search restricted to tools. Distinct from the cross-catalogue /search.

parameters.name: runtime
parameters.description: How the tool executes: node, python, or http.

post.description: Upsert-shaped: creates the tool on first publish and appends an immutable version thereafter. The endpoint the CLI targets.

runtime.description: How the tool executes: node, python, or http.

input_schema.description: A JSON Schema document describing the payload shape.

output_schema.description: A JSON Schema document describing the payload shape.

post.description: Resolves a version or range to a concrete published version and returns the full manifest plus its dependency tools, so a client can materialise the tool locally in one round trip.

version.description: Exact version or semver range. Defaults to the latest published version.

delete.description: Soft-deletes the tool. Refused with 409 while any agent still declares it as a dependency.

get.summary: List published versions of a tool
Normalized runtime view of a published agent
get.description:
Parses the manifest, detects the runtime, resolves every declared tool, and reports
which environment variables are satisfied.

Pass `?environment=A,B` to ask whether the agent could run with those variables set.
Anything unsatisfied appears in `environment.missing` and in `diagnostics`, and
`ready` becomes false.

parameters.name: environment
parameters.description: Comma-separated environment variable names available to the agent.

post.summary: Validate and normalize an agent.json before publishing
post.description:
The pre-flight a CLI runs before `norien publish`. Validates the manifest, detects the
runtime, resolves tool dependencies against the registry, and reports what publishing
this version would do via `version_check.action`:

- `create` — the slug is free
- `new_version` — the version would be accepted
- `conflict` — the version is already published or lower than the current latest

Structural problems return 422. A manifest that parses but cannot be satisfied returns
200 with `ready: false` and the reasons in `diagnostics`.

environment.description: Environment variable names (or a name/value map) available to the agent.

slug.description: Slug to check the version against. Derived from `name` when omitted.

get.description: Ranked full-text search across both catalogues. Results carry a relevance `score` and a `type` discriminant, so agents and tools can be shown in one merged list.

parameters.name: strategy
parameters.description: Preferred ranking strategy. Falls back when unavailable.

post.description: Resolves the requested version or range against what has been published, records the installation, and returns everything needed to run the agent -- including its resolved tool dependencies. Repeated calls are idempotent.
Tombstones the active installation, preserving install history.

Upsert-shaped publishing endpoint intended for the CLI: creates on first publish and appends an immutable version thereafter. `type` may be omitted when the payload is unambiguous (a `manifest` implies an agent; `input_schema` implies a tool).

get.description: Ranked token listing from the primary market-data provider. Defaults to the native chain; pass `chainId` for another.
Market data, metadata, and on-chain identity for one token, merged into a single normalized record.
DeFi protocols ranked by TVL, filterable by chain, category, and name.
One protocol with its per-chain TVL breakdown, plus repository health when it links a GitHub project.
Verified source, ABI, creator, and token identity for a contract, combining the explorer with a direct node read.
Balance, nonce, recent transactions, and token transfers for an address on the native chain.
Searches tokens and projects together. An input that looks like an address resolves directly to that address.
Current block height and gas price, read directly from the node.
Which external providers are configured, whether each is reachable right now, and live cache statistics.

tags.name: Tools
tags.description: Reusable capabilities that agents depend on.

tags.name: Publishing
tags.description: Unified publish endpoint for the CLI.
